Name: The Beatles
Bio: The most influential band in history, the Fab Four from Liverpool redefined popular music and culture in the 1960s.

Name: Radiohead
Bio: Pioneers of experimental rock, known for their evolving sound and critical acclaim from 'OK Computer' to 'Kid A'.

Name: Oasis
Bio: The definitive Britpop band of the 90s, led by the Gallagher brothers and known for anthems like 'Wonderwall'.

Name: Nirvana
Bio: The icons of Grunge, led by Kurt Cobain, who brought alternative rock into the mainstream with 'Nevermind'.

Name: Queen
Bio: Legendary rock band fronted by Freddie Mercury, famous for their theatrical performances and genre-bending hits.

Name: The Doors
Bio: Led by Jim Morrison, they were a central part of the 60s psychedelic rock scene with their dark and poetic sound.

Name: David Bowie
Bio: The ultimate musical chameleon, Bowie was a pioneer of glam rock and a visionary who constantly reinvented his persona.

Name: Bon Jovi
Bio: New Jersey's stadium rock kings, known for their infectious hooks and power ballads since the mid-80s.

Name: My Chemical Romance
Bio: The definitive emo-rock band of the 2000s, known for their theatrical concepts and loyal fan base.

Name: Pixies
Bio: Influential alternative rock pioneers whose loud-quiet-loud dynamic inspired countless bands including Nirvana.

Name: Arctic Monkeys
Bio: Leading the indie rock revival of the 2000s, known for Alex Turner's sharp lyrics and their evolving style.

Name: Eric Clapton
Bio: The only three-time inductee to the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, a master of blues-rock guitar.

Name: Jeff Beck
Bio: A virtuoso guitarist who pushed the boundaries of rock, jazz fusion, and blues with his innovative technique.

Name: Grand Funk Railroad
Bio: A popular American hard rock band of the 70s, known for their energetic live shows and 'Americana' sound.

Name: Rainbow
Bio: Formed by Ritchie Blackmore, this hard rock powerhouse featured legendary vocalists like Ronnie James Dio.

Name: George Harrison
Bio: The 'Quiet Beatle' who introduced Indian music to the West and had a successful, spiritually-driven solo career.

Name: RADWIMPS
Bio: A versatile Japanese rock band famous for their emotional soundtracks for Makoto Shinkai's animated films.

Name: King Gnu
Bio: A contemporary Japanese quartet blending rock, jazz, and pop into a unique 'Tokyo Chaotic' sound.

Name: the pillows
Bio: An influential Japanese alternative rock band, widely known for their music in the anime series FLCL.

Name: WANIMA
Bio: An energetic Japanese punk rock trio known for their positive lyrics and catchy melodies.

Name: Frank Sinatra
Bio: The legendary 'Chairman of the Board,' one of the most popular and influential musical artists of the 20th century.

Name: Louis Armstrong
Bio: A foundational figure in jazz, 'Satchmo' was a virtuoso trumpeter and an influential singer with a gravelly voice.

Name: Count Basie
Bio: A giant of the Big Band era, he led one of the most swinging jazz orchestras in history for over 50 years.

Name: George Benson
Bio: A virtuoso guitarist and soulful singer who successfully bridged the gap between jazz and R&B.

Name: Ben E. King
Bio: A soul and R&B singer best known as the lead singer of The Drifters and for his solo hit 'Stand by Me'.

Name: The Drifters
Bio: A long-running vocal group that defined the doo-wop and early soul sound with hits like 'Under the Boardwalk'.

Name: Erik Satie
Bio: An eccentric French composer and pianist whose minimalist 'Gymnopédies' paved the way for ambient music.

Name: Frédéric Chopin
Bio: The master of Romantic piano music, known for his poetic and technically demanding compositions.

Name: Ludwig van Beethoven
Bio: A crucial figure in the transition between the Classical and Romantic eras, one of the greatest composers ever.

Name: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky
Bio: The most popular Russian composer of all time, famous for his grand ballets like 'The Nutcracker'.

Name: BBC Symphony Orchestra
Bio: A world-class orchestra founded in 1930, central to the UK's cultural life and the BBC Proms.

Name: Maurizio Pollini
Bio: A legendary Italian pianist known for his intellectual depth and technical perfection in classical repertoire.

Name: Mikhail Pletnev
Bio: A Russian virtuoso pianist, conductor, and composer, and the founder of the Russian National Orchestra.

Name: Paul Lewis
Bio: A leading English pianist, celebrated for his profound interpretations of Beethoven and Schubert.

Name: Jean-Guihen Queyras
Bio: A French cellist known for his versatility, performing everything from Baroque to contemporary music.

Name: Jiří Bělohlávek
Bio: A renowned Czech conductor who was a champion of Czech music and led major orchestras worldwide.

Name: Jóhann Jóhannsson
Bio: An influential Icelandic composer whose work blended classical orchestration with ambient electronic sounds.

Name: SEATBELTS
Bio: The eclectic band formed by Yoko Kanno, famous for the jazz and blues-infused soundtrack of Cowboy Bebop.

Name: Kanye West
Bio: A visionary rapper, producer, and fashion designer who has consistently pushed the boundaries of hip hop.

Name: Nas
Bio: One of the greatest lyricists in hip hop history, whose debut 'Illmatic' is considered a masterpiece.

Name: Mac Miller
Bio: A beloved rapper and producer whose musical journey evolved from frat-rap to introspective and soulful jazz-rap.

Name: Lupe Fiasco
Bio: A highly technical and conscious rapper known for his complex lyricism and philosophical themes.

Name: Kid Cudi
Bio: A pioneer of melodic and introspective hip hop who has influenced a generation of 'emo-rap' artists.

Name: Ice Cube
Bio: A key figure in gangsta rap, from his days with N.W.A. to a successful solo career in music and film.

Name: JAŸ-Z
Bio: A hip hop titan and billionaire businessman, one of the most successful and influential rappers of all time.

Name: Pusha T
Bio: The king of 'coke-rap,' known for his cold delivery and intricate wordplay since his Clipse days.

Name: Killer Mike
Bio: An activist and rapper, one half of Run the Jewels, known for his powerful social commentary and flow.

Name: Raekwon
Bio: A legendary Wu-Tang Clan member, the 'Chef' whose debut 'Only Built 4 Cubalinx...' defined cinematic rap.

Name: RZA
Bio: The mastermind behind the Wu-Tang Clan, a revolutionary producer who defined the gritty sound of 90s NYC rap.

Name: André 3000
Bio: One half of Outkast, an eccentric and brilliantly talented rapper and multi-instrumentalist.

Name: Nujabes
Bio: A Japanese producer who pioneered the 'lo-fi hip hop' sound by blending jazz and soulful beats with rap.

Name: Shing02
Bio: A bilingual Japanese-American rapper known for his thoughtful lyrics and collaboration with Nujabes.

Name: Young Thug
Bio: A revolutionary trap artist known for his eccentric vocal style and avant-garde fashion sense.

Name: Future
Bio: The king of modern trap, known for his prolific output and pioneering use of melody and Auto-Tune.

Name: Rick Ross
Bio: The 'Boss' of Miami hip hop, known for his booming voice, luxury-focused lyrics, and cinematic production.

Name: Lil Baby
Bio: One of the most dominant voices in modern rap, known for his melodic flow and chart-topping consistency.

Name: Nicki Minaj
Bio: The 'Queen of Rap,' a versatile artist who blended hard-hitting rap with pop sensibility and eccentric personas.

Name: Swizz Beatz
Bio: A powerhouse producer and DJ who defined the sound of the early 2000s with his high-energy 'Ruff Ryders' beats.

Name: DOBERMAN INFINITY
Bio: A Japanese hip hop and R&B group known for their energetic tracks and part of the Exile Tribe collective.

Name: EXILE THE SECOND
Bio: A powerful J-pop and R&B performance group, an elite sub-unit of the legendary Exile collective.

Name: The Weeknd
Bio: A global superstar who transitioned from mysterious PBR&B to chart-topping dark pop and synth-wave.

Name: Daniel Caesar
Bio: A soulful Canadian singer-songwriter known for his smooth vocals and contemporary take on classic R&B.

Name: H.E.R.
Bio: A multi-talented R&B singer and virtuoso guitarist, known for her mysterious persona and soulful ballads.

Name: John Legend
Bio: A multi-platinum artist and EGOT winner, known for his soulful voice and piano-driven romantic hits.

Name: Joss Stone
Bio: A British soul singer who rose to fame with her powerful, gravelly voice and vintage R&B sound.

Name: CeeLo Green
Bio: A versatile artist known for his soulful voice, flamboyant style, and the global hit 'Crazy' with Gnarls Barkley.

Name: Summer Walker
Bio: A leading voice in contemporary R&B, known for her vulnerable lyrics and atmospheric production.

Name: Kali Uchis
Bio: A bilingual artist who blends R&B, reggaeton, and neo-soul into a dreamy and nostalgic sound.

Name: Omar Apollo
Bio: A rising star who blends indie-pop, R&B, and soul, singing in both English and Spanish.

Name: Akon
Bio: A global R&B and hip hop icon who dominated the 2000s with his unique voice and catchy hooks.

Name: T-Pain
Bio: The pioneer of creative Auto-Tune use in modern R&B and hip hop, a gifted singer and songwriter.

Name: Ty Dolla $ign
Bio: A prolific singer and producer, the go-to collaborator in modern R&B and hip hop for his smooth melodies.

Name: PARTYNEXTDOOR
Bio: A central figure in the 'OVO' sound, known for his moody, atmospheric R&B and songwriting for major stars.

Name: Dwele
Bio: A key figure in the Detroit neo-soul scene, a multi-instrumentalist known for his smooth and jazzy R&B.

Name: Eryn Allen Kane
Bio: A Chicago-based soul singer with a powerful, gospel-inflected voice and a classic retro sound.

Name: serpentwithfeet
Bio: An avant-garde R&B artist whose music blends soulful vocals with experimental and queer gospel influences.

Name: Lola Young
Bio: A rising British talent with a raw, husky voice and honest songwriting that blends pop and soul.

Name: Mustafa
Bio: A Canadian singer-songwriter who creates 'folk-soul,' blending somber melodies with stories of his community.

Name: Bon Iver
Bio: Justin Vernon's project that evolved from intimate indie folk to experimental art-pop and electronic soundscapes.

Name: Rex Orange County
Bio: An English singer-songwriter known for his sunny indie-pop sound and candid lyrics about love and youth.

Name: BADBADNOTGOOD
Bio: An instrumental quartet from Toronto who blend jazz virtuosity with hip hop and soul sensibilities.

Name: YOASOBI
Bio: The 'unit that turns novels into music,' a J-pop duo that became a global phenomenon with their high-energy sound.

Name: Jun Togawa
Bio: A Japanese cult icon known for her avant-garde music, eccentric vocal style, and unique stage presence.

Name: Ngọt
Bio: The leading indie band in Vietnam, known for their poetic lyrics and a sound that blends rock with pop.

Name: Chillies
Bio: A popular Vietnamese indie-pop band known for their catchy melodies and emotional ballads.

Name: Vũ.
Bio: The 'Prince of Indie' in Vietnam, famous for his deep voice and melancholy ballads about love and memories.

Name: Cá Hồi Hoang
Bio: An influential Vietnamese indie rock band known for their conceptual albums and energetic live shows.

Name: Đen
Bio: The most popular rapper in Vietnam, known for his humble persona, deep lyrics, and relatable storytelling.

Name: AMEE
Bio: The 'Queen of Teen Pop' in Vietnam, known for her sweet voice, bright image, and series of viral hits.

Name: GREY D
Bio: A multi-talented Gen Z artist known for his emotional songwriting, smooth R&B vocals, and unique musicality.
